channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Remembering Siphiwe Tshabalala’s Historic Goal and the Boots Behind It
item.description: Almost 16 years ago, Siphiwe Tshabalala made South African football history by scoring the opening goal of the 2010 World Cup, thrilling a nation eager to witness Bafana Bafana on the global stage. Wh...

item.title: Maldives Cave Diving Tragedy Claims Five Lives, Including Experienced Instructors and Scientists
item.description: A tragic diving accident occurred in the Maldives when five Italians, including an experienced diving instructor and four scientists, died while exploring the Thinwana Kandu cave, also known as Shark ...

item.title: US Pushes G7 Countries to Increase Sanctions on Iran as Economic Pressures Rise
item.description: The United States, through Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, has called on G7 nations to join efforts in tightening sanctions against Iran. The call comes amid ongoing tensions from the Middle East co...

item.title: Punjab authorities warn of rain, dust storms and thunderstorms till Saturday
item.description: The Provincial Disaster Management Authority (PDMA) of Punjab has issued a weather alert, forecasting rain, dust-thunderstorms, and occasional hail in multiple districts from Monday night until Saturd...

item.title: A Retrospective on Brian Jones: Music, Life, and Legacy
item.description: Brian Jones (1942–1969) was a pioneering English musician and the founder of The Rolling Stones. Known for his multi-instrumental talent, he introduced unique instruments such as the sitar, marimba, a...

item.title: Philippine Lotto Results for May 18, 2026
item.description: ABS-CBN News reports the official lotto results for May 18, 2026, as announced by the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO). The article provides the latest numbers for various lotto games, ena...

item.title: Research Finds Rising Number of Fabricated References in Medical Publications
item.description: A recent study published in The Lancet has revealed a concerning rise in fabricated references in medical research papers. Researchers, led by Maxim Topaz, Ph.D., from Columbia University, developed a...

item.title: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda a Public Health Emergency
item.description: World Health Organization (WHO) has officially declared the Ebola outbreak affecting Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda as a public health emergency of international concern. This outbreak ...

item.title: Australia’s AI budget: Moving beyond generic chatbots to specialised solutions
item.description: Australia’s 2026 federal budget promises to boost productivity through artificial intelligence (AI), but experts warn that relying on generic chatbots is not enough for meaningful economic gains. Smal...

item.title: Three members of a family die in alleged domestic violence incident in Campbelltown
item.description: A tragic incident in Campbelltown, south-west Sydney, has left a woman in her mid-40s and her two children, both under 10, dead following an alleged domestic violence attack. Police were called after ...

item.title: How Silent Book Clubs Are Helping Canadians Reconnect with Reading
item.description: After the pandemic disrupted daily routines, many Canadians found it difficult to focus on reading. Silent book clubs have emerged across the country as a solution, providing a low-pressure environmen...

item.title: Trump Plans Permanent Helipad on White House South Lawn Citing Helicopter Needs
item.description: President Donald Trump is planning another major change to the White House grounds by installing a permanent helipad on the South Lawn, according to a Wall Street Journal report. This follows numerous...

item.title: Uganda enforces new law limiting foreign influence on politics
item.description: On Monday, Uganda’s President Yoweri Museveni signed into law the 'Protection of Sovereignty' bill, aiming to curb foreign influence in the country. The legislation comes after parliament softened som...

item.title: How Two Traders Manipulated the 1955 Chicago Onion Market and Prompted a Federal Ban
item.description: In 1955, Vincent Kosuga, an onion farmer, and Sam Siegel, a businessman, orchestrated one of the most infamous market manipulations in U.S. history by cornering nearly the entire onion supply in Chica...

item.title: Key Economic Indicators Affecting Stocks and Commodities in 2026
item.description: Dis year 2026, anyone investing in stocks or commodities must pay attention to different economic signals dat affect markets. Inflation is a big one; high inflation can make stock prices drop and caus...

item.title: Nvidia Shows Strong Revenue Growth and Attractive Valuation Despite High Market Cap
item.description: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) continues to attract investor attention due to its remarkable growth metrics and valuation. Trading at approximately 26 times forward earnings with a 65.47% revenue increase in t...

item.title: Judge Rules Evidence from Backpack Cannot Be Used in Health Insurance CEO Murder Trial
item.description: A New York state court judge has ruled that evidence found in Luigi Mangione’s backpack during his arrest cannot be used in his upcoming murder trial. Mangione, 28, faces charges for the December 2024...

item.title: Senate Begins Impeachment Proceedings Against VP Sara Duterte
item.description: On May 18, 2026, the Philippine Senate formally convened as an impeachment court to hear the case against Vice President Sara Duterte, following her second impeachment by the House of Representatives....

item.title: African Fintech Shows Signs of Recovery and Investment Potential
item.description: African fintech is experiencing a resurgence in investor interest, driven by the continent's unique financial challenges and growing market opportunities. In late 2025, South Africa's Optasia and Moro...
